description: User request → generation brief (inference); sets form contract for SRPO poet

templates.default:
A poet has asked for help. Their request:

"{user_request}"

The poet model you are briefing was trained with strong rhyme and revision supervision (SRPO-style): it follows explicit form contracts well when you spell them out. Vague form language produces weak drafts.

Construct a COMPACT generation brief (~300 tokens max). Include:
- Angle (2-3 sentences — a real angle, not the default take on the topic)
- Clichés to avoid (5-6 specific phrases tied to this topic)
- Imagery domain (1-2 sentences — concrete, not generic "nature")
- Form contract: If they named or implied a fixed form (sonnet, villanelle, terza rima, etc.), state line count, stanza structure, and the exact rhyme scheme labels (e.g. ABAB…). If they asked for rhyme without naming a form, still specify scheme or rhyme pairs. If free verse, say so explicitly.
- Hard constraints vs soft preferences (what must not be violated vs what is negotiable)
{style_ctx}No rhetorical flourish. No poem draft — instructions only. Actionable only.
